evals.prompt: Write Terraform to provision a production-ready AWS setup for a Node.js API: an ECS Fargate cluster, an RDS PostgreSQL database, an Application Load Balancer, and a VPC with public and private subnets. The app should be in private subnets, the ALB in public subnets, and the database in private subnets.
evals.expected_output: Terraform code with modular structure: VPC (public/private subnets), ECS Fargate cluster + task definition + service, RDS PostgreSQL in private subnet, ALB in public subnet, security groups, IAM roles, and outputs for the ALB endpoint.
expectations: Code includes VPC with both public and private subnets
Code includes ECS Fargate cluster and service configuration
Code includes RDS instance in private subnet with db_subnet_group
Code includes Application Load Balancer in public subnets
Security groups restrict database to only allow app access
Database password uses random_password or Secrets Manager (not hardcoded)
Code includes variables.tf with descriptions and outputs.tf

evals.prompt: I need Terraform for a simple S3-hosted static website with CloudFront CDN in front. The S3 bucket should be private and only accessible via CloudFront. Include a custom domain configuration placeholder.
evals.expected_output: Terraform for S3 bucket (private ACL), CloudFront distribution with Origin Access Control, bucket policy allowing only CloudFront, and optional ACM certificate + Route53 record for custom domain.
expectations: S3 bucket has block_public_acls and block_public_policy enabled
CloudFront distribution uses Origin Access Control (OAC, not legacy OAI)
Bucket policy allows only CloudFront to read objects
CloudFront is configured for SPA routing (custom error for 404 → index.html)
Custom domain/ACM certificate section is included (even if as commented or variable)
